text.title: Django Coding Practices Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: ViewSet method runs 70 lines: parses request, validates, writes to 3 tables, sends email, renders response.
antipattern.root-cause: No service layer; everything lives in the view.
antipattern.fix: Extract logic into model methods or a service module; view becomes <30 lines: parse + call service + render.

antipattern.symptom: Page loads slowly; django-silk shows 1 + N queries where N matches table size.
antipattern.root-cause: Template loops over qs and accesses obj.fk.field without select_related/prefetch_related.
antipattern.fix: Add .select_related('fk') for FKs and .prefetch_related('m2m_field') for M2Ms in the view's qs.

antipattern.symptom: On payment failure, the Order row is created but the PaymentAttempt row is missing; state inconsistent.
antipattern.root-cause: Two .save() calls without transaction.atomic; second one raised.
antipattern.fix: Wrap the whole flow in transaction.atomic (decorator on service method or context manager block).

antipattern.symptom: User reports an unexpected email after registration; tracing reveals a post_save signal three apps away.
antipattern.root-cause: In-app side-effect implemented via signal; flow is implicit.
antipattern.fix: Move side-effect into explicit service function or model.save() override. Reserve signals for true cross-app concerns.

antipattern.symptom: settings.py contains hard-coded DEBUG=True under an if branch; secrets visible in git.
antipattern.root-cause: No env-aware split; team patched the file per environment.
antipattern.fix: Split into base/dev/prod + DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE env var. Load secrets via django-environ from .env (gitignored).
